title: Error when trying to start engine
body:
### Issue Description

Every 4-8 hours all my container stop working but are still show as running.
It is not possible to start them without restarting the engine itself.
Restarting the engine results in another error that is typically shows 5-10 times and then it starts working again. 

Error when starting engine:
```
podman machine start podman-machine-default
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users:/Users
Error: exit status 255
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

podman machine start podman-machine-default

### Describe the results you received

```
podman machine start podman-machine-default
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users:/Users
Error: exit status 255
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the engine to start immediately without error

### Additional information

comments.body:
@doberkofler I'm wondering about a couple of things given your issue:  Would you mind providing a little more feedback?

1. Have you ever tried `podman machine stop` before trying the start?
2. When you reported earlier that the containers are reported to be running, how are you checking that? `podman ps` from the host operating system?
3. Any chance the networking is dorked up or there is a major time gap between host and machine?
4. If you `podman machine ssh`, does that work?  And if so, anything in journalctl that might reveal what is going on?
5. Do the container logs reveal any hints?
6. Does `podman --log-level=debug` reveal anything?

Thank you for caring! After trying podman for a few weeks I just gave up as it seems to very unstable in my environment and switched back to colima.

> 1. Have you ever tried `podman machine stop` before trying the start?

Yes. I always use `podman machine stop` before `start`.

> 2. When you reported earlier that the containers are reported to be running, how are you checking that? `podman ps` from the host operating system?

I run an Oracle Database in the container. Typically after 1-2 hours podman stops working as noticed by the database no longer accepting requests. When I then look to the dashboard the container seem to be running but I cannot restart them.
Did not try `ps`. The machine seems to be running as well but when trying to stop the machine it hangs. I have to kill all process to stop it. When then trying to restart the reported error happens. Sometimes it helps to try a few times but mostly I have to remove the and init the machine.

> 3. Any chance the networking is dorked up or there is a major time gap between host and machine?

Possible but not likely. I just switched back to colima and use the same images that work as expected.

> 4. If you `podman machine ssh`, does that work?  And if so, anything in journalctl that might reveal what is going on?

Did not try. There is no `journalctl` on macOS but ratger `log`.
```
log show --predicate 'process == "com.github.containers.podman"'

Filtering the log data using "process == "com.github.containers.podman""
Skipping info and debug messages, pass --info and/or --debug to include.
Timestamp                       Thread     Type        Activity             PID    TTL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Log      - Default:          0, Info:                0, Debug:             0, Error:          0, Fault:          0
Activity - Create:           0, Transition:          0, Actions:           0
```

> 5. Do the container logs reveal any hints?

I was never able to access the container logs.

> 6. Does `podman --log-level=debug` reveal anything?

Did not try.

I think it's a dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17403

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17403#issuecomment-1536636874 for a workaround
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
If this is a dup, I am closing.
